roofline.method: Exact tile storage: BF16=2048, BFP8_B=1088, BFP4_B=576 bytes per 32x32 tile. Count all TP4 attention/router/norm weights, four globally selected EP experts, and 128 BF16 K/V tokens. Divide aggregate bytes by 4 * 512 GB/s Blackhole DRAM bandwidth.

same_profiled_run.named_gap: 0.082939 ms/token (14.45%) between merged device op time and the short three-replay wall window. It contains trace execute/dispatch, start/end synchronization, profiler instrumentation, and host timing. The CSV's merged cross-device op-to-op gaps overlap and are not additive to critical-path device time.

separate_unprofiled_headline_run.fixed_overhead_amortization_evidence: The production timing uses 500 warmed trace replays and is only 0.012213 ms above the profiled device-op estimate. It is reported separately and is not substituted into the same-run table.

limitations: The roofline is a lower bound for compulsory weight and KV reads; it omits activation, CCL, routing, and small-op launch costs.
Dynamic expert ownership can load-imbalance devices even though the aggregate selected-expert byte count is fixed.
The three-replay profiler window intentionally limits CSV volume, so its fixed synchronization and instrumentation cost is less amortized than the headline 500-replay run.
